title: Experimenting with LLM-based trading via Alpaca. Looking for others working on similar systems.
author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
selftext:
I was early to bitcoin in 2013, made enough that mid career I'm taking time off to build trading bots that use AI to make decisions. I can't retire quite yet, but I can take some time to work on this project and hopefully keep it humming when I go back to the corporate world.



Timeline:



2023 - Started vibecoding (no coding background). Built small personal tools and experiments.



January 2026 - Built a paper trading bot using ChatGPT and Alpaca that was trending toward 300% APY after 6 weeks, 100+ trades, figured this was a good enough proof of concept to turn on real money. 



February 2026 - Turned on options and live money (small amount for proof of concept), lost about 30% over a month. The spreads on the bid / asks for options on Alpaca was death by 1000 paper cuts. I had 3 competing bots with slightly different strategies, 2/3 lost and one gained only modestly. I had recursive learning loops, agent personalities, weekly automated upgrades, it was a mess and way over complicated. I was having too much fun vibecoding and built a monstrosity.



April 2026 - Scrapped version 1 and built a version 2 that's using Grok instead of chatGPT to make decisions. I see Grok is out competing other models usually in trading competitions, so going with that. I stripped it to be as simple as possible. It's trading defined-risk vertical credit spreads on SPY (Grok's idea, I'm not a technical trader), but basically just betting on direction of SPY with defined risk. This way I don't get killed on bid / ask spreads since there's more liquidity on SPY options. So far it's up modestly over 11 days. SPY has been up significantly during that time, so until it makes money shorting on down days it's hard to get overexcited.



Link to dashboard here: [https://grok-trading-dashboard.vercel.app](https://grok-trading-dashboard.vercel.app)



AMA, happy to share what I've learned, curious what others are building. I know there's a spark of something here and I see no reason you can't wire up an alpaca account to an LLM and print money if you get the right pieces in place. And more importantly, these LLMs will only get smarter month by month.

  
  
Currently playing around with variables and happy to share what I've learned or help others get there. Vibecoding is way easier than most people realize.

comments.author: Effective_Appeal_736
comments.body:
Really interesting breakdown — especially the part where paper trading looked amazing and then execution killed it live. That seems to be the common pattern.

I’ve been building something similar (LLM + backtesting + paper trading), and honestly I ran into the same realization: most of the “edge” disappears once you factor in spreads, slippage, and real fills. The model itself isn’t really the advantage — it’s more about how you structure the system and control risk.

Your move to simplify and focus on SPY makes a lot of sense. Curious — have you tried modeling execution costs directly in your backtests now? That’s the piece I’m working on next because it feels like that’s where most systems quietly break.

Also agree on the overengineering trap… super easy to end up with a monster instead of something robust.

replies.author: Evilpostman
replies.body:
Literally where I’m at too. Paper trading was great then live trading has been atrocious. 

OP: happy to collab on set ups but I have the same type of set up. Openclaw, Claude sonnet and alpaca api with the bot pulling live news all day with the local llm to avoid fees.

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body:
I'm currently using cowork (opus 4.7) -> grok -> alpaca. Cowork just pings a grok thread using chrome. I just do one run per day but it's making money live trading. 10AM is decision time, buy sell or hold. Grok searches to make the decision and so far so good. Super simple. 

Alpaca is $100 / mo, cowork I could get down to $20/month, so $120 per month is the hurdle for this small business to make sense to keep running. If it works I have a lot of cash I could pile in, but need it to prove itself first.

replies.author: jeidndond
replies.body: Are you willing to share source code ?

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body: Nothing to share for code. It’s LLMs doing the heavy lifting not parameters. The only code I have is for the dashboard to watch the trades

replies.author: jeidndond
replies.body: just so I understand, you use Cowork -> grok -> alpaca?

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body: Yup. Cowork has a scheduled run, it gathers market data, pings grok, grok decides on a trade or to sit, cowork executes with alpaca.

comments.author: Appreciationize818
comments.body: the options bid-ask spread problem on Alpaca killed a version of mine too, around the same time you were running yours. i was trading 0DTE SPY calls and the fill slippage was basically a flat tax on every trade. switching to SPY credit spreads like you did is the right move, the tighter markets make the model's edge actually show up in realized pnl instead of disappearing at execution. curious whether grok is making intraday reads or just EOD

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body:
One read per day at 10AM. Buy sell or hold. No decisions until the next day. Super simple. My V1 I did 10AM, 1PM and 3:30PM, but switched to just 1 run per day.

I'm using a scheduled instance of cowork to drive alpaca... I could save $100 / month and avoid these lousy fills having cowork navigate the fidelity UI. Just feels like a lot more room for error that way. I partitioned an amount I'm comfortable losing to Alpaca for now. 

Were you making lots of trades? It's day 13 and it just entered it's 3rd trade. The way the trades are structured there's limited up side and down side so I'm no too worried about intraday trading. 

https://grok-trading-dashboard.vercel.app

I guess just making this the simplest functionally possible for now. The set up took me like 4 hours start to finish and then made a few tweaks here and there after launch.

replies.author: Evilpostman
replies.body: How do you get this dashboard? Through grok?

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body:
I vibecoded it and hosted it on vercel. Part of the instruction in the cowork schedule is to maintain the trade log file on the dashboard. 

Basically anything you're trying to do you can just explain to cowork in a prompt. "I want to be able to check in on this from my phone or any computer or share with friends, how can I do that."
